journal_entry.reason: The $125 meal for factory production staff is classified as manufacturing overhead, which is a product cost that will be allocated to inventory. Since it was paid with a corporate credit card, we debit Manufacturing Overhead (increasing the overhead pool to be allocated to production) and credit Credit Card Payable (increasing the liability owed to the credit card company).

output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction involves a meal expense for factory production staff classified as manufacturing overhead. When manufacturing overhead is incurred, it is debited to Manufacturing Overhead (an asset/inventory-related account that will eventually be allocated to Work in Process and finished goods). The payment was made using a corporate credit card, which creates or increases a liability (Credit Card Payable). This is a straightforward transaction: debit Manufacturing Overhead (increases an asset/expense account) and credit Credit Card Payable (increases a liability).
